decks.notes: This deck revolves around the powerful enchantment Oath of Druids, which allows for rapid graveyard play. It aims to control the game by disrupting the opponent's mana base with cards like Stone Rain and Wasteland, while using creatures like Terravore and Treetop Village to apply pressure. The deck also includes utility spells like Naturalize and Pyroclasm to deal with threats efficiently. Overall, the strategy is to outmaneuver the opponent with resource denial and strong creatures, ultimately overwhelming them with a combination of control and aggression.

The main concept of this deck is to establish a lock on the opponent by combining cards like Stasis and Forsaken City with counter spells and bounce effects. This creates a situation where the opponent is unable to untap their lands and effectively stops them from playing spells, while also dealing damage with cards like Black Vise. The deck aims to control the game and eventually win by overwhelming the opponent with the lock in place.

This deck focuses on using enchantments to create a powerful engine, with Argothian Enchantress as the key card drawing engine. The deck utilizes various enchantment cards to ramp up mana production, draw more cards, and control the board. With cards like Exploration and Enchantress's Presence, the deck can quickly assemble its combo pieces and overwhelm the opponent. Replenish is used to bring back crucial enchantments from the graveyard, while Opalescence and Parallax Wave provide win conditions. The deck aims to outvalue and outgrind the opponent through enchantment synergy.

The deck relies on utilizing cards like Attunement and Frantic Search to quickly fill the graveyard with enchantments, then using Replenish to bring them all back onto the battlefield. The key concept revolves around creating a powerful board state with Opalescence turning all enchantments into creatures, while controlling the opponent's board with Parallax Tide and Parallax Wave. Stifle can be used to disrupt the opponent's strategy, while cards like Swords to Plowshares and Seal of Cleansing provide removal and utility. The mana base is built to support the deck's color requirements and provide consistent access to the necessary lands.

This deck focuses on utilizing Hermit Druid to mill cards from the library and fill the graveyard, enabling powerful combos with cards like Karmic Guide, Phyrexian Devourer, and Necrotic Ooze. The deck also includes ways to protect and recur key combo pieces, such as Coffin Purge and Shallow Grave. Multiple ways to tutor for combo pieces and disrupt the opponent's hand with cards like Cabal Therapy make this deck a versatile and powerful combo strategy in the Premodern format.

The main focus of this deck is aggressive play with a combination of powerful creatures and efficient removal spells. By utilizing cards like Lightning Bolt and Terminate alongside creatures like Hypnotic Specter and Grim Lavamancer, the deck aims to quickly reduce the opponent's life total while disrupting their hand with cards like Duress and Cabal Therapy. The deck's mana base is designed to support this aggressive strategy, with a mix of powerful lands like Mishra's Factory and efficient dual lands to ensure consistent mana production for the deck's spells.

The main focus of this Mono Black Control deck is to disrupt and control the opponent's resources through a combination of discard spells, removal, and land destruction. By utilizing cards like Cabal Therapy, Duress, Innocent Blood, and Contamination, the deck aims to keep the opponent on the back foot while generating advantage with cards like Nether Spirit and Skeletal Scrying. Engineered Plague is used as a board control tool, while Zombie Infestation can generate a swarm of tokens. The deck also includes land disruption with Wasteland and Rishadan Port to further disrupt the opponent's game plan.

This deck focuses on utilizing resource denial and aggressive creatures to quickly take control of the game. By using cards like Terravore and Armageddon, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's mana base while building up a strong board presence. Weathered Wayfarer helps find important lands like Wasteland or Kor Haven, while Meddling Mage can shut down key spells from the opponent. The deck also includes some card draw with Sylvan Library and creature tokens with Call of the Herd. Overall, the strategy revolves around creating a fast and efficient offense while hindering the opponent's resources.

The main focus of this Mono Blue Control deck is to maintain control of the game by countering opponent's spells, bouncing threats back to hand, and drawing cards to maintain advantage. The deck utilizes powerful cards like Counterspell, Powder Keg, and Fact or Fiction to control the board and disrupt the opponent's game plan. The deck also includes powerful finishers like Morphling and Mishra's Factory to close out the game once the opponent's resources have been depleted. Overall, this deck aims to outlast the opponent and win through attrition and control.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive Goblin creatures, supported by cards that help accelerate the deck's strategy. The key goal is to establish an early board presence and apply pressure to the opponent, utilizing cards like Goblin Lackey and Goblin Matron to bring in powerful Goblins from the deck. The deck also includes removal spells like Gempalm Incinerator to deal with opposing threats and maintain control of the battlefield. The mana base is designed to support the deck's aggressive plays and ensure consistent access to the necessary colors of mana.

This Mono Black Aggro deck focuses on using aggressive creatures like Priest of Gix, Cabal Archon, and Rotlung Reanimator to quickly overwhelm the opponent. The deck also utilizes disruption spells like Duress, Cabal Therapy, and Smother to disrupt the opponent's game plan. With a mix of creature recursion and removal spells, the deck aims to maintain board control and pressure the opponent into submission. Efficient use of mana acceleration with cards like Dark Ritual and Rishadan Port allows the deck to quickly deploy threats and disrupt the opponent's strategy.

The focus of this mono blue control deck is on disruption and card advantage, with a strong emphasis on countering opponent's spells and controlling the board through various means such as Parallax Tide and Powder Keg. The deck also includes mana denial with cards like Wasteland and Dust Bowl. The win condition is through creature beatdown with Mishra's Factory and Faerie Conclave, or through combo with Phyrexian Dreadnought. Overall, the deck aims to outlast and outmaneuver the opponent while efficiently drawing cards and controlling the game.

The deck revolves around quickly filling up the graveyard with enchantments using cards like Attunement and Frantic Search, then casting Replenish to bring them all back onto the battlefield. This creates a powerful combo with Opalescence turning those enchantments into creatures and Parallax Wave and Tide providing control elements. Orim's Chant and Seal of Cleansing offer disruption and protection while the mana base provides consistent access to the necessary colors. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a large board presence and win the game quickly.

The main idea of this deck is to utilize the powerful combo between Replenish and Opalescence to quickly wipe out opponents' creatures and enchantments while deploying your own threats. By using cards like Attunement and Frantic Search to fill up your graveyard, you can set up for a game-winning Replenish to bring back all of your enchantments at once. Mana denial and disruption from cards like Mana Leak and Seal of Cleansing help to protect your combo pieces and maintain control of the game.

The deck focuses on aggressive plays with a strong emphasis on dealing damage quickly to the opponent. It utilizes low-cost and efficient creatures like Jackal Pup and Mogg Fanatic, backed up by burn spells such as Lightning Bolt and Incinerate. The deck also includes disruptive elements like Seal of Fire and Naturalize to handle opposing threats. The mana base supports a fast tempo with lands like Forest','','');">Karplusan Forest and Treetop Village, while providing some disruption with Wasteland. Overall, the deck aims to apply constant pressure and overwhelm the opponent with a fast and efficient clock.

This deck focuses on quickly overwhelming the opponent with aggressive creatures and burn spells. The goal is to deal as much damage as possible in the early game, using efficient creatures like Jackal Pup and Mogg Fanatic alongside burn spells like Lightning Bolt and Incinerate. Price of Progress punishes opponents for playing non-basic lands, while Sulfuric Vortex ensures consistent damage each turn. The deck aims to close out the game with burn spells like Fireblast and Reckless Abandon, finishing off the opponent before they have a chance to stabilize.
